name: Sidapa
description: Sidapa is a Visayan deity who dwells atop Mount Madia-as in Antique, Philippines. He is primarily known as the god of death and the one who measures the lives of mortals. It is believed that Sidapa has a towering tree on the mountain, where he notches each person's life span. When the notch breaks, the individual dies. He is also associated with beauty, masculinity, and the moon. Some stories depict him as having a rivalry with Bulan, the moon god, due to Bulan's exceptional beauty surpassing his own. In some versions, Sidapa seeks to possess Bulan, leading to various conflicts. His role is crucial in maintaining the balance between life and death, and his presence is a constant reminder of mortality. He is sometimes invoked in rituals related to health and longevity, although his primary association remains with death and the afterlife. He is a powerful and enigmatic figure in Visayan mythology, embodying the inevitable end that awaits all living beings.
subtitle: Visayan God of Death and Measurement

keyMyths.title: Sidapa and the Notched Tree
keyMyths.description: Sidapa measures the lives of humans by making notches on a massive tree on Mount Madia-as. When a notch breaks, the person's life ends, signifying their death.

keyMyths.title: Sidapa and Bulan's Rivalry
keyMyths.description: Sidapa, envious of Bulan's unparalleled beauty, desires to possess him, leading to a conflict between the two lunar deities. This rivalry is a common theme in Visayan tales.
keyMyths.source: Gregorio Zaide, Philippine History and Civilization

keyMyths.title: Sidapa's Domain on Mount Madia-as
keyMyths.description: Mount Madia-as is considered Sidapa's sacred domain, a place where mortals rarely venture due to its association with death and the supernatural presence of the deity.

sources.author: F. Landa Jocano
